_comment: Automated maps loop. Generates islands (world maps) and indoor zones (caves, houses) via the PixelLab API. A 'zone' is one loading zone = one subfolder under maps/. TILESETS (Wang, two seamlessly-connecting terrain levels) and OBJECTS (transparent props) are shared assets generated at most once under assets/; a zone references them and copies the tiles/props it uses so each zone folder is self-contained. worldgen builds a corner-height grid; zone.py bakes it into a tile-index grid + collision + preview. See maps/README.md and maps/spec/MAPS_SPEC.md.
style_base: cohesive top-down RPG pixel-art overworld in the style of Grave Seasons / Stardew Valley, warm naturalistic palette, soft readable shapes, gentle shading

scale._comment: Proportion contract enforced by pipeline/proportions.py so characters walk at a believable size. A person is ~1x2 tiles (Grave Seasons/Stardew look). The characters domain draws bodies ~67px tall, so 32px tiles put a character at ~2.1 tiles tall (correct); 16px would make it ~4 tiles (too big). Every object's px size = tiles x tile_size, so props are always in scale.

budget._comment: Shared PixelLab pool across characters/objects/maps agents (see coordination/PROTOCOL.md). Maps reserves a 2000-generation floor so it never starves the other domains; the loop stops cleanly below it.

_tilesets: Wang tilesets: lower/upper are two terrain levels that connect seamlessly; the loop generates each once and reuses it across zones. transition_size 0 = 16 tiles (plain boundary); 0.25/0.5/1.0 add a raised transition band (more tiles).

tilesets.lower: deep blue ocean water with gentle ripples

tilesets.upper: dense dark green forest floor with ferns
plastered interior wall with wooden beams

_objects: Transparent props. `on` lists terrain levels they may scatter onto; `blocks` marks them impassable (added to collision). `tiles` is the object's REAL footprint in tiles — pixel size is derived as tiles x tile_size (proportions.py), so every prop is in scale with a ~1x2-tile character (a barrel ~1 tile, an oak ~3, a cottage ~4).

objects.description: a lush round-canopy oak tree seen from above
a tall pointed pine tree seen from above
a small round green leafy bush
a small cluster of colorful wildflowers
a mossy grey boulder
a leaning tropical palm tree seen from above
a short wooden pier dock
a small cozy cottage with a thatched roof seen from above
a large two-story house with a red tiled roof seen from above
a round grey stone water well
a small stone campfire with burning logs
a small wooden signpost
a wooden barrel
a wooden storage crate
a closed wooden treasure chest with iron bands
a pointed grey rock stalagmite
a glowing blue crystal cluster
a wooden bed with a quilt seen from above
a round wooden table seen from above
a tall wooden bookshelf full of books
a woven decorative rug seen from above

_zone_plan: Built in order; small islands first (as requested). Each zone references tilesets by id in `bands` (band[k] draws the boundary between levels[k] and levels[k+1]) and picks objects from the pool. `links` wire an exit to another zone (loading zone).

zone_plan.title: Isle of Dawn
zone_plan.description: A small quiet starter island of beach and meadow, no settlement.

zone_plan.title: Whispering Isle
zone_plan.description: A small wooded island ringed by beach; a forest crowns its centre.

zone_plan.title: Harbor Town
zone_plan.description: A larger island with a small harbour town of cottages.

zone_plan.title: Hollow Cave
zone_plan.description: A small rocky cave interior, one exit.

procedural_zones._comment: When zone_plan is exhausted, the loop invents more small islands by cycling these axes (seed derived from index). Keeps producing fresh world maps forever.
